div1.entryFree: Scandal, vb. to defame, to bring into disgrace --ed the suppliants for the people, called them time pleasers, Cor. III, 1, 44. hug them hard and after s. them, Caes. I, 2, 76. Sinon's weeping did s. many a holy tear, Cymb. III, 4, 62. Partic. --ed == scandalous, disgraceful: her and her blind boy's --ed company, Tp. IV, 90.
